...
At IQVIA, I lead the end-to-end delivery of AI solutions for clinical research, taking projects from proof-of-concept through to secure production deployment. I collaborate with engineers, data scientists, and clinical SMEs to translate complex requirements into scalable frameworks that accelerate clinical reporting. By applying multi-agent orchestration and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines, I automate the synthesis of clinical study documents, cutting timelines and reducing manual effort. I leverage the latest AI/ML stack — including LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Azure, HuggingFace, Docker, and Terraform — to deliver efficient and reliable solutions. These efforts drive organisational adoption and support global rollout strategies through close engagement with stakeholders. Show less
...
#### Machine Learning Engineer Data Scientist
...
Project: Defeat COVID-19 As the lead Machine Learning Engineer in a high-profile international clinical study, I spearheaded data science initiatives aimed at exploring an anti-inflammatory drug's potential to alleviate moderate to severe COVID-19 symptoms. My role was pivotal in leveraging both Large Language Models (LLMs) and statistical analysis methods to meticulously evaluate patient outcomes, enhancing our understanding of the drug's effectiveness across diverse patient demographics. I significantly advanced our research capabilities by optimizing a Large Language Model (LLM). This involved pretraining on the extensive MIMIC-IV Electronic Health Record (EHR) dataset, followed by fine-tuning with our proprietary clinical data. This strategic approach resulted in a remarkable 16% improvement in the model's F1 score, thereby surpassing the original model's predictive accuracy and setting a new benchmark for our predictive analytics. Furthermore, I was instrumental in designing, developing, and deploying a sophisticated end-user interactive tool. This innovation enabled comprehensive mapping of multi-type data—ranging from textual and numerical to images—into a cohesive 2-dimensional space. This tool proved invaluable for conducting thorough assessments of clinical biases and patient similarities, ultimately enhancing our data's integrity. A significant breakthrough came when this tool helped identify data falsification at one hospital site, leading to the exclusion of 34 patients from our study. This critical intervention ensured a more accurate and reliable evaluation of the drug's efficacy, reinforcing the study's integrity and contributing to the broader scientific community's efforts to combat COVID-19.
